result:
I'd make curiosity the default setting for humanity.

Not the polite, hobby-level kind, but the relentless, slightly obsessive version—the one that makes people stay up too late because they need to understand how something actually works. If that became the norm instead of status, comfort, or tribal signaling, most of the stupid, cruel, and self-destructive things we do would start looking ridiculous by comparison. People would rather figure out the next layer of reality than fight over the current one.

message.reasoning_content: If I could change the world in one way, I'd make the pursuit of truth the central organizing principle of human society. That way, there'd be no more politics of division or echo chambers—just a collective drive to figure out what's really going on in this universe.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
